title: ShaderGraph: Nodes, Properties, Keywords, Subgraphs
summary: Visual shader authoring via ShaderGraph: master-stack graphs (Lit / Unlit / Fullscreen / Fog) that compile to URP or HDRP shaders only. Covers the node-category map, exposed properties vs. keywords, subgraphs for reuse, Custom Function for HLSL escape hatches, and graph settings (precision, surface, blend). Use before any mosaic shadergraph/* tool call or when generating authored shaders (shader/parallax-create, shader/sss-create) that must drop into a URP project. Key gotcha: ShaderGraph has no Built-in target — assigning a graph-authored shader to a Built-in project renders pink.

llmGuidance.when_asked_to_create_a_new_shader_on_urp: Prefer shadergraph/create with ShaderType='Lit' unless the surface is explicitly unlit (FX, UI, skybox). The generated .shadergraph carries a master stack that already targets the active URP/HDRP pipeline — do not hand-author HLSL unless a Custom Function is required.
llmGuidance.when_the_active_pipeline_is_Built-in: ShaderGraph does NOT support Built-in. Do not create a .shadergraph; the resulting material renders pink. Fall back to a hand-written .shader (Standard / legacy surface shader) or direct the user to install URP. See kb-rendering-pipeline-detection to confirm the pipeline first.
llmGuidance.when_exposing_a_material_parameter: Add a ShaderGraph Property (not a Keyword) and mark it Exposed. Supported types: Texture2D / Texture2DArray / Texture3D / Cubemap, Color (with HDR flag for emission), Float, Vector2-4, Boolean, Matrix4. The Property's Reference string (e.g. '_BaseColor') is what code uses with Material.SetColor / SetFloat / SetTexture — always set Reference explicitly so it survives graph refactors.
llmGuidance.when_adding_a_toggle_to_a_graph: Use a Boolean Keyword (shader variant) only if the branch affects code generation or the optimizer can eliminate dead samplers. Otherwise use a Float property + Branch node — no variant explosion. A Boolean Keyword doubles the variant count; four booleans = 16 variants = slow compiles and big builds.
llmGuidance.when_adding_an_enum_toggle: Use an Enum Keyword with 2-4 entries. Past 4 entries the variant count becomes painful. For >4 options prefer a Float property + Switch node unless each branch contains cost-asymmetric code that benefits from static dispatch.
llmGuidance.when_factoring_reusable_node_chains: Create a Subgraph (Asset > Create > Shader Graph > Sub Graph). Give it typed input/output ports and Exposed Properties for anything the parent must drive. Good candidates: custom BRDF helpers, triplanar mapping, UV warps, noise stacks. Subgraphs compile into the parent — no runtime cost vs. inlining.
llmGuidance.when_graph_nodes_cannot_express_the_math: Drop in a Custom Function node. Two modes: File (point at an .hlsl file with a named function) or String (inline HLSL). Prefer File mode for anything longer than ~10 lines — it survives copy/paste and gets diff-friendly version control.
llmGuidance.when_shader_property_default_needs_updat: Call shadergraph/set-property-default with the graph path and the Property Reference string. This mutates the .shadergraph asset directly (JSON edit). For runtime value changes, use material/set-property against an instance instead — don't touch the graph asset at runtime.
llmGuidance.when_debugging_'graph_renders_pink': Four causes ranked by frequency: 1) active pipeline is Built-in (no ShaderGraph target); 2) target pipeline mismatch (HDRP graph in a URP project or vice versa); 3) keyword combination was stripped from the build; 4) subgraph output not connected to anything. Check pipeline first.
llmGuidance.when_precision_artifacts_appear_at_large: Graph defaults to Single (fp32) precision per node, but nodes can be set to Half (fp16) for perf. If position/UV math uses Half at far world coordinates, you'll see stepping. Fix by setting Graph Settings > Precision to Single globally, or selectively on the affected nodes.

full.overview: ShaderGraph is a node-based shader authoring tool distributed as a Unity package (com.unity.shadergraph). It targets Scriptable Render Pipelines only — URP and HDRP. A graph compiles into a Unity .shader through a code generator whose output depends on the master stack, the connected nodes, and the graph's declared keywords. Nothing in a graph runs in the Built-in Render Pipeline; attempting to convert a graph for Built-in requires re-authoring against legacy surface shaders.

masterStack.concept: The master stack is the graph's terminal node — the point where color, normal, alpha, and other surface outputs are written. Replaces the pre-10.x 'Master Node' with two typed blocks (Vertex stack + Fragment stack) you can add or remove block nodes on.

targets.name: Lit
targets.use: PBR surfaces. Drives base color, metallic, smoothness, normal, emission, occlusion.

targets.name: Unlit
targets.use: Self-illuminated or stylized surfaces. No lighting. FX, UI overlays, skyboxes.

targets.name: Fullscreen
targets.use: Post-process and blit effects via Fullscreen Pass Renderer Feature. Operates in screen space.

masterStack.stacks: Vertex stack receives Position / Normal / Tangent blocks. Fragment stack receives Base Color / Normal / Metallic / Smoothness / Emission / Alpha / Alpha Clip Threshold / etc. Only the blocks you connect are written — unconnected blocks default.

nodeCategories.summary: Graph palette is organized into categories. The LLM doesn't need to remember individual node names — it needs to know which category to reach for.

categories.use: Properties, Geometry (Position, Normal, UV, Tangent), Scene (Time, Screen, Camera), Matrix constants.
Arithmetic, trig, vector ops, interpolation, range remap, derivatives. The biggest category.
Color adjustments (hue/saturation, levels), blend modes, dither, posterize, contrast.
Gradient noise, Simplex noise, Voronoi, checkerboard. For hand-made textures without sampler cost.
Flipbook, Rotate, Tile+Offset, Twirl, Polar Coordinates, Parallax (heightmap offset).
Preview, Branch (if), Custom Function, Subgraph, Keyword, Redirect.
Sample Texture 2D / 2D Array / 3D / Cubemap, Sampler State, Texel Size, Triplanar.

properties.concept: Properties appear in the Material Inspector when Exposed, and are settable at runtime via Material.SetColor / SetFloat / SetTexture using the property's Reference string (e.g. '_BaseColor'). Unexposed properties behave as graph-local constants with a default value.

types.inspector: Toggle — NOTE: implemented as a float 0/1, not a keyword unless marked as Keyword

properties.referenceStringRule: Always set Reference explicitly (e.g. '_BaseColor') before shipping. If left auto-generated, it becomes a random GUID-like string that breaks downstream code that does material.SetColor('_BaseColor', ...).

keywords.concept: Keywords generate distinct shader variants at compile time — each keyword combination produces a separate compiled shader. Useful for dead-code elimination of cost-asymmetric branches (e.g. with/without normal map sampling). Dangerous because the variant count is multiplicative.

kinds.notes: On/Off. Enable at runtime with material.EnableKeyword / DisableKeyword. Global keywords share across materials; Local keywords are per-material.
Multiple named states (e.g. _QUALITY_LOW / _MED / _HIGH). Set exactly one active per material.

keywords.variantExplosion: Four boolean keywords = 2^4 = 16 variants. Six = 64. URP/HDRP also add pipeline-scoped keywords on top (shadows, GI, XR, etc.), multiplying further. Build-time shader compilation scales with the product. Rule: budget 3-4 graph-authored keywords max per shader. Convert borderline cases to Float + Branch node (no variant, runtime dynamic branch).

subgraphs.concept: A Sub Graph is a reusable fragment of graph logic with typed input ports and a single Output node. Consumers drop a Subgraph node into their main graph; Unity inlines the subgraph's body during shader code generation.
useCases: Shared custom BRDF computation used by multiple Lit graphs.
Triplanar sampling helper (accepts base texture + scale + blend sharpness, outputs blended color + normal).
Noise stacks (fBM of Gradient Noise at multiple octaves) authored once and reused.
UV warps (polar, radial, scrolling, displaced) factored out of main graph.
Preserving visual clarity in a big graph by folding logical sections behind a single node.
rules: Sub Graph Output node accepts multiple typed outputs. Connect them in the parent graph as needed.
Exposed properties on a sub graph become ports on the consumer node — they do NOT appear in the consumer material's inspector. Propagate through the parent graph.
Sub graphs can reference other sub graphs but must not cycle.

customFunctionNode.concept: Escape hatch for HLSL that the node palette cannot express — custom lighting, loops, external includes, platform intrinsics. The function is pasted into the generated shader at the node's position.

modes.use: Point at an .hlsl asset and specify a function name. Preferred for >10 lines or code under version control.
Inline snippet. Use for 1-2 liners and prototypes.

signatureRules: Inputs declared on the node become function parameters in declared order.
Outputs declared on the node become 'out' parameters.
Function name on the node must match the HLSL function name exactly.
Precision (half/float) must be consistent with the graph's precision setting or explicitly annotated.

graphSettings.summary: Graph-level settings control compile targets and surface behavior. Accessed via the Graph Inspector > Graph Settings tab.

keySettings.notes: A graph can target both pipelines simultaneously; each target produces a separate compiled sub-shader.

keySettings.options: Lit, Unlit, Fullscreen, Fog, Decal, Canvas
keySettings.notes: Determines which master stack blocks are valid.
Transparent adds Blend settings and removes depth write by default.
Enables Alpha Clip Threshold block in fragment stack.
Opaque default on, transparent default off.

decisionRules.scenario: Creating any new non-trivial shader on URP or HDRP
decisionRules.rule: Use shadergraph/create with ShaderType='Lit' (or 'Unlit' for FX). Do not hand-author HLSL unless Custom Function is needed for something the nodes cannot express.
decisionRules.evidence: ShaderGraph is Unity's supported URP/HDRP authoring surface; hand-written .shader files bypass the target system and tend to break on pipeline upgrades.

decisionRules.scenario: Target project uses Built-in Render Pipeline
decisionRules.rule: Do not emit a .shadergraph. Fall back to a hand-authored Standard or legacy surface shader; warn the user ShaderGraph is URP/HDRP-only.
decisionRules.evidence: ShaderGraph package explicitly excludes a Built-in target. Output assigned to Built-in project renders pink.

decisionRules.scenario: Graph needs a toggle between two cost-asymmetric branches
decisionRules.rule: Use a Boolean Keyword so the optimizer strips the disabled branch. Budget total keyword count ≤ 4 (including enum keywords weighted by N-1).
decisionRules.evidence: Variants multiply: 4 bool keywords = 16 variants; plus pipeline variants, build-time compile grows fast.

decisionRules.scenario: Graph needs a toggle between two cheap branches
decisionRules.rule: Use Float property + Branch node. No shader variant, runtime dynamic branch on a GPU-friendly scalar. Saves compile time.
decisionRules.evidence: Modern GPUs handle uniform-branch predication cheaply; variant explosion is the bigger risk in content-heavy projects.

decisionRules.scenario: Same lighting math reused in multiple graphs
decisionRules.rule: Factor into a Sub Graph. Update once, propagates to all consumers. Use typed ports and Exposed Properties for driven parameters.
decisionRules.evidence: Sub Graphs inline at compile time; no runtime cost vs. inlining.

commonPitfalls.symptom: Material using a .shadergraph renders pink in the scene.
commonPitfalls.cause: Active pipeline is Built-in (no target) OR graph targets a different pipeline than the project (HDRP graph on URP project, or vice versa).
commonPitfalls.fix: Confirm pipeline via GraphicsSettings.currentRenderPipeline. Ensure Graph Settings > Active Targets includes the project's pipeline. For Built-in projects, abandon ShaderGraph.

commonPitfalls.symptom: Build time balloons and player shader memory is huge.
commonPitfalls.cause: Keyword explosion — boolean/enum keywords multiply pipeline-provided keywords.
commonPitfalls.fix: Replace runtime-cheap toggles with Float + Branch node. Reduce enum keyword entries. Target ≤ 4 graph-authored keywords per shader.

commonPitfalls.symptom: material.SetColor('_BaseColor', ...) has no effect on a ShaderGraph material.
commonPitfalls.cause: Property's Reference string is not '_BaseColor' — likely the auto-generated GUID-looking default.
commonPitfalls.fix: Open the graph, select the property, set Reference explicitly. Re-save. Verify with shadergraph/get-properties.

commonPitfalls.symptom: Surface looks coarse / stepped at far world positions.
commonPitfalls.cause: Precision mismatch — Half (fp16) used on position or UV math where fp32 was required.
commonPitfalls.fix: Set Graph Settings > Precision to Single globally, or flip the offending nodes to Single precision.

commonPitfalls.symptom: Sub Graph preview looks correct but consumer graph outputs black.
commonPitfalls.cause: Sub Graph's Output node has unconnected ports, or the consumer didn't connect the sub graph node's output.
commonPitfalls.fix: Every port on the Sub Graph Output must drive from somewhere inside the sub graph; consumer must connect the sub graph node to a master stack block or downstream node.

versionDeltas.change: Master Node replaced by Master Stack (Vertex + Fragment block stacks). Old graphs auto-upgrade on open. Target system introduced — one graph can now compile to URP and HDRP simultaneously.
versionDeltas.migrationNote: Graphs from earlier versions upgrade automatically; review block connections after upgrade.

versionDeltas.change: Fullscreen master stack target added for URP post-process / blit. Canvas target added for UI. Unified URP/HDRP master stack improvements.
Unity 6 series. Stable master stack, no breaking API changes for existing graphs. URP Fullscreen Pass Renderer Feature becomes the standard consumer of Fullscreen graphs.

authorshipNotes: Content authored by Mosaic team from publicly-documented Unity ShaderGraph package APIs, the ShaderGraph user manual, and open-source ShaderGraph package source (BSD-family licensed Unity-Technologies/Graphics). Node category names, master stack target names, property type names, and graph setting enum values are factual data (not copyrightable). Explanatory prose, decisionRules, llmGuidance, commonPitfalls, and the variant-explosion budget heuristic are original Mosaic editorial content. Unity Manual and package documentation pages are cited as externalReferences and NOT reproduced here.
